name: Texas Instruments Incorporated
summary: Texas Instruments Incorporated (TXN, Information Technology) — composite stance BULLISH. Latest reported quarter FY2026 Q1: revenue $3.92B (+22.2% YoY), net margin 39.4%. Valuation 29.0x forward P/E (+41% vs peers). Spot $309.21 (above 200-DMA).

bull: Top-line momentum — revenue grew +22.2% YoY in FY2026 Q1 to $3.92B, with TTM revenue of $14.72B confirming the run-rate. Growth at this pace is unusual for a company of this scale and reflects either share gains, pricing power, or new-product cycles depending on the line-item mix in the Fundamental tab.

Profitability premium — net margin of 39.4% in the latest quarter sits well above the cross-sector ~10–15% benchmark, indicative of either structural pricing power or operating leverage from scale. Expect this to compress only modestly through-cycle.
Margin trajectory — net margin expanded +9.9pp over the last 4 quarters in the dataset. Management's cost-out commentary on recent calls is showing up in the P&L, not just the press release.

Trend regime constructive — spot $309.21 sits +54.0% above the 200-DMA ($200.73). The long-term moving average has been a reliable reference for institutional capital and remains supportive of the directional bias.
bear: Momentum stretched — RSI at 70 in overbought territory. Near-term mean-reversion risk elevated; consider waiting for a pullback toward the 50-DMA ($242.04) before adding.

Valuation premium — 29.0x forward P/E vs peer median of 20.5x — a 41% premium. The setup requires continued out-execution to maintain; any reversion to peer-median multiples compresses the stock meaningfully even before any earnings revision.
52-week extension — at 100% of the 52-week range, the stock is one bad print away from a 10–15% reset back into the middle of the range. Position size accordingly.

Put skew elevated — ATM put IV running +20.4pp above call IV. The options market is paying up for downside protection — a contrarian green-flag in extreme cases, but more often a leading indicator of impending news.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 25% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Net margin expands above 40.9% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
Clean breakout above $309.21 (52-week high) on volume confirmation — technical signal that institutional accumulation is sustained.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 19% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Net margin compresses below 37.4% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $153.33 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line:
BULLISH. Texas Instruments Incorporated (TXN) printed $3.92B in revenue in FY2026 Q1 (+22.2% YoY), net margin of 39.4%. At 29.0x forward P/E, the stock carries a 41% premium to peers (20.5x median) — the multiple needs continued execution to defend.

Technical regime is mixed: trend is intact above the 200-DMA ($200.73) but RSI 70 signals near-term overbought conditions — consider entry timing. Sell-side consensus PT of $195 implies -36.9% upside. 30-day implied move is ±11.6%; 90-day is ±24.2%. Position-sizing should reflect conviction; use the trigger list to manage adds and trims around the next earnings catalyst.
